points.description: System Initiate Start-up Sequence (Return to Service)

Islanded Mode.  Determines how the DER behaves when in an Islanded configuration.
    <0>  Isochronous Mode.  DER attempts to control voltage and frequency independent of configured curves and settings up to the limits of the machine's capabilities in order to achieve AO reference voltage and AO nominal frequency.
    <1> Droop Mode.  DER acts as a follower using Volt/Var and Freq/Watt curves.

Enable Sensed Grid Config Detection.  If Enabled, the DER may independently change its Active Settings Group based on locally observed grid conditions.
    <0> No Autonomous Detection.
    <1> Autonomous Detection.  Inverter's Active Settings Group  may differ from the Requested Settings Group
Storage Capacity Units.  Determines whether the energy storage values are expressed in Amp-hrs or Watt-hrs.
Time Constant Mode.  Indicates whether Time Constant Ramp parameters are interpreted as Open Loop Response times or 3Tau values.
Enable Dynamic Reactive Current Support Mode
Enable Active Power Following Mode #1 (Peak Power Limiting)
Enable Active Power Following Mode #2 (Load Following)
Enable Active Power Following Mode #3 (Generation Following)

Enable Event-Based Reactive Current Support Mode.  The moving average voltage and the base reactive current are frozen until after the voltage has returned to within the deadband for a specified hold time.  Dynamic Reactive Current Support mode must be Enable for this setting to apply.
Frequency-Watt Curve Mode - Snapshot of Power
Charge/Discharge Mode - Use Ramp Rates. Indicates whether or not Charge/Discharge should use specified ramp rates or time constants.
AGC Mode - Use Ramp Rates. Indicates whether or not AGC mode should use specified ramp rates or time constants.
Volt-Watt - Use Ramp Rates and Time Constants.  Indicates whether Volt-Watt mode should use only Time Constants, or both Time Constants and Ramp Rates
Volt-Var Enable Autonomous Voltage Reference Adjustment
Enable Frequency-Watt Droop Function. Use Watts per Hz

Storage State of Charge at Maximum.  Maximum Usable State of Charge Reached.
Storage State of Charge is Too High. Maximum Reserve Percentage (of usable capacity) reached.
Storage State of Charge is Too Low.  Minimum Reserve Percentage (of usable capacity) reached.
Storage State of Charge is Depleted.  Minimum Usable State of Charge Reached.
Storage Internal Temperature is Too High
Storage External (Ambient) Temperature is Too High

System Is In Local  State.  System has been locked by a local operator which prevents other operators from executing commands.  Note: Local State is also sometimes referred to as Maintenance State. Local State overrides Lockout State.
System Is In Lockout State.  System has been locked by an operator such that other operators may not execute commands. Lockout State is also sometimes referred to as Blocked State
System Is Starting Up.  Set to 1 when a BO "System Initiate Start-up Sequence" command has been received.
System Is Stopping.  Set to 1 when an BO "System Execute Stop" command has been received.
System is Started (Return to Service).  If any of the DER Units are started, then true.  DER Units in the maintenance operational state are excluded.
System is Stopped (Cease to Energize).  If all of the DER Units are stopped, then true.  DER Units in the maintenance operational state are excluded.
DER is Off but Available to Start
DER is Off and Not Available to Start

Islanded Mode.  Determines how the DER behaves when in an Islanded configuration.
    <0>  Isochronous Mode.  DER attempts to control voltage and frequency independent of configured curves and settings up to the limits of the machine's capabilities in order to achieve the AO Reference Voltage and AO nominal frequency.
    <1> Droop Mode.  DER acts as a follower using Volt/VAR and Freq/Watt curves.

Sensed Grid Config Detection Enabled.  If Enabled, the DER may independently change its Active Settings Group based on locally observed grid conditions.
    <0> No Autonomous Detection.
    <1> Autonomous Detection.  Inverter's Active Settings Group may differ from the Requested Settings Group

Open Loop Response Time Percentage.  Percent of target to reach within the open loop response time.  Default is 90%.

     <3> Percent of Available Reactive Power (VArAval)

Requested Island Configuration.  Enumeration:
    <0> Not Used
    <1> Unspecified / Autonomously Determined (see BO Enable Sensed Grid Config Detection)
    <2> Factory Configuration
    <3> Default Configuration / Comms Lost
    <4> Normal Grid-Connected Configuration
    <5> Islanded Condition 1 (small, local island)
    <6> Islanded Condition 2 (larger, area island)
    <7> Islanded Condition 3 (largest, regional island)
    <8> 1st Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <9> 2nd Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <10> 3rd Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <11-255> Reserved for future assignment

Islanded Configuration Being Edited.  Enumeration:
    <0> Not Used
    <1> Unspecified / Autonomously Determined (see BO Enable Sensed Grid Config Detection)
    <2> Factory Configuration
    <3> Default Configuration / Comms Lost
    <4> Normal Grid-Connected Configuration
    <5> Islanded Condition 1 (small, local island)
    <6> Islanded Condition 2 (larger, area island)
    <7> Islanded Condition 3 (largest, regional island)
    <8> 1st Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <9> 2nd Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <10> 3rd Alternate Grid-Connected Configuration
    <11-255> Reserved for future assignment
Freeze Counter Interval. Interval between freeze counter operations after the initial occurrence.  A zero value means the free counter operation is not repeated.

Freeze Counter Interval Units. Units of the interval between freeze counter operations:
   <0> The outstation does not repeat the action, regardless of the Interval count.
   <1> Milliseconds - In this case the interval is always counted relative to the Start Time and is constant regardless of the clock time set at the Outstation.
   <2> Seconds - At the same millisecond within the second that is specified in the Start Time.
   <3> Minutes - At the same second and millisecond within the minute that is specified in the Start Time.
   <4> Hours - At the same minute, second and B7millisecond within the hour that is specified in the Start Time.
   <5> Days - At the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time.
   <6> Weeks - On the same day of the week at the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time
   <7> Months - On the same day of each month at the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time. If the Start Time falls on the 29th or greater day of the month, the outstation shall not perform the action in months that do not have such a day
   <8> Months on Same Day of Week from Start of Month - At the same timeof day on the same day of the week after the beginning of the month as the day specified in the Start Time.
For instance, if the Start Time specifies the second Tuesday of
February and the Interval Count is 2, the next action shall occur on the second Tuesday of April. In the same example, if the Interval Count is set to 12, this is the same as specifying, “Every year on the second Tuesday in February”. If the specified day does not occur in a given month when an action was scheduled to occur, the outstation shall not perform the action that month but shall perform it at the next valid scheduled time.
   <9> Months on Same Day of Week from End of Month - The outstation shall interpret this setting as in <8>, but the day of the week shall be measured from the end of the month, e.g.,“the second-last Tuesday in February”.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Signal Meter ID.  Referenced ECP.  This is the meter from which current is being read to evaluate and provide support.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the voltage is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the voltage is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Signal Meter ID.  Referenced ECP.  This is the meter from which current is being read to evaluate and provide support.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through High Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the frequency is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Low Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the frequency is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Mode Priority
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Enabling Time Window
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Enabling Ramp Time
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Reversion Timeout Period
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Signal Meter ID

Dynamic Reactive Current Support - Gradient Mode.  Enumeration:
     <0> Undefined
     <1> Gradients reach 0 at the moving average Voltage
     <2> Gradients reach 0 at the Voltage deadbands

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Deadband Minimum Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.Vref), measured from the moving average voltage (RDGS.VAv).  Support is no longer applied when the voltage stays above this value for the length of the Hold Time.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Deadband Maximum Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.Vref), measured from the moving average voltage (RDGS.VAv).  Support is no longer applied when the voltage stays below this value for the length of the Hold Time.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Gradient for Sags.  Percentage of the rated current (DRAT.ARtg) to apply capacitively per percentage of the negative deviation from the moving average voltage (RDGS.Av).   It is a ratio of percent and is therefore unitless.
points.units: percent current per percent voltage deviation

points.description: Dynamic Reactive Current Support Gradient for Swells. Percentage of the rated current (DRAT.ARtg) to apply inductively per percentage of the positive deviation from the moving average voltage (RDGS.Av).   It is a ratio of percent and is therefore unitless.
points.units: percent current per percent voltage deviation

points.description: Dynamic Reactive Current Support Filter Time for Moving Average Voltage (RDGS.VAv).  Used to determine amount of dynamic reactive current support.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Block Zone Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.VRef) below which no reactive current support shall be applied.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Hysteresis Block Zone Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.VRef).   After being blocked, reactive current support shall not resume until the voltage has been above BlkZnV + HysBlkZnV.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Block Zone Time.  Time in milliseconds from the beginning of any "sag" event, before which dynamic reactive current support will always continue, regardless of how low voltage may sag.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Start Hold Time.  When the voltage exceeds the deadband limits for this length of time (measured in milliseconds), the "sag" or "swell" event begins and the DER may begin altering active power output.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support End Hold Time.  When the voltage returns to within the deadband limits for this length of time (measured in milliseconds), the "sag" or "swell" event is considered to be over.  Reactive current support ends, frozen values are unfrozen, and a new event can begin.

Dynamic Volt-Watt Gradient.  Signed unit-less quantity that establishes the ratio of additional Watts supplied (expressed in terms of % DRCT.WMax) to the present difference from the moving average voltage (expressed as % DRCT.VRef).
points.units: percent watts per percent voltage difference

points.description: Dynamic Volt-Watt Filter Time.  The time in seconds used to calculate the moving average voltage for dynamic Volt-Watt support.

Dynamic Volt-Watt Lower Deadband.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.Vref) measured below the moving average voltage.  If the present voltage is above this value, no additional Watts shall be supplied.

Dynamic Volt-Watt Upper Deadband.  Percentage of the nominal voltage (DRCT.Vref) measured above the moving average voltage.  If the present voltage is below this value, no additional Watts shall be supplied.

points.units: percent watts per percent hz difference

Curve Edit Selector.  Writing to this point selects which of the curves  can currently be viewed and changed.

Curve Type.  Enumeration:

 <0> Curve is not defined
 <1> Not applicable / Unknown
 <2> Volt-Var 
 <3> Frequency-Watt
 <4> Watt-Var 
 <5> Voltage-Watt 
 <6> Remain Connected
 <7> Temperature mode
 <8> Pricing signal mode

High Voltage ride-through curves
 <9> HVRT Must Trip 
 <10> HVRT Momentary Cessation 
 
Low Voltage ride-through curves
 <11> LVRT Must Trip 
 <12> LVRT Momentary Cessation 

High Frequency ride-through curves
 <13> HFRT Must Trip 
 <14> HFRT Momentary Cessation

Low Frequency ride-through curves
 <15> LFRT Must Trip 
 <16> LFRT Momentary Cessation

Independent (X-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:

     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / Unknown
     <4> Time (milliseconds)
     <23> Celsius Temperature
     <29> Voltage (V)
     <33> Frequency (Hz)
     <38> Watts (W)
     <100> Price in hundredths of local currency
     <129> Percent Voltage
     <133> Percent Frequency
     <138> Percent Watts
     <233> Frequency Deviation
     <234+> Other

Dependent (Y-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:
     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / unknown
     <2> Vars as percent of max Vars (VarMax)
     <3> Vars as percent of max available Vars (VarAval)
     <4> Vars as percent of max Watts (Wmax)  - not used
     <5> Watts as percent of max Watts (Wmax)
     <6> Watts as percent of frozen active power (DeptSnptRef)
     <7> Power Factor
     <8> Volts as a percent of the nominal voltage (VRef)
     <9> Frequency as a percent of nominal frequency

Freeze Counter Interval. interval between freeze counter operations after the initial occurrence.  A zero value means the free counter operation is not repeated.

Freeze Counter Interval Units. Units of the interval between freeze counter operations:
   <0> The outstation does not repeat the action, regardless of the Interval count.
   <1> Milliseconds - In tInverters case the interval is always counted relative to the Start Time and is constant regardless of the clock time set at the
Outstation.
   <2> Seconds - At the same millisecond witInvertern the second that is specified in the Start Time.
   <3> Minutes - At the same second and millisecond witInvertern the minute that is specified in the Start Time.
   <4> Hours - At the same minute, second and B7millisecond witInvertern the hour that is specified in the Start Time.
   <5> Days - At the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time.
   <6> Weeks - On the same day of the week at the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time
   <7> Months - On the same day of each month at the same time of day that is specified in the Start Time. If the Start Time falls on the 29th or greater day of the month, the outstation shall not perform the action in months that do not have such a day
   <8> Months on Same Day of Week from Start of Month - At the same timeof day on the same day of the week after the beginning of the month as the day specified in the Start Time.
For instance, if the Start Time specifies the second Tuesday of
February and the Interval Count is 2, the next action shall occur on the
second Tuesday of April. In the same example, if the Interval Count is
set to 12, tInverters is the same as specifying, “Every year on the second
Tuesday in February”.
If the specified day does not occur in a given month when an action
was scheduled to occur, the outstation shall not perform the action that
month but shall perform it at the next valid scheduled time.
   <9> Months on Same Day of Week from End of Month - The outstation shall interpret tInverters setting as in <8>, but the day of the week shall be measured from the end of the month, e.g.,“the second-last Tuesday in February”.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Signal Meter ID.  Referenced ECP.  TInverters is the meter from which current is being read to evaluate and provide support.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Voltage Reference Input.  Active voltage measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is High.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is low.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation / discharging must stop when the voltage is High.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation / discharging must stop when the voltage is low.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Signal Meter ID.  Referenced ECP.  TInverters is the meter from which current is being read to evaluate and provide support.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Frequency Reference Input.  Active frequency measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is High.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is low.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through High Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies where generation / discharging must stop when the frequency is High.
Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Low Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies where generation / discharging must stop when the frequency is low.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Mode Priority
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Enabling Time Window
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Enabling Ramp Time
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Timeout Period
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Signal Meter ID.  Referenced ECP meter from which current is being read to evaluate and provide support.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Voltage Reference Input.  Votltage measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Moving Average Voltage
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Present Delta Voltage.  Difference in Volts between the present measured Voltage and the Moving Average Voltage as a percentage of the reference voltage

Dynamic Reactive Current Support - Gradient Mode.  Enumeration:
     <0> Undefined
     <1> Gradients reach 0 at the moving average Voltage
     <2> Gradients reach 0 at the Voltage deadbands

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Deadband Minimum Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage, measured from the moving average voltage.  Support is no longer applied when the voltage stays above tInverters value for the length of the Hold Time.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Deadband Maximum Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage, measured from the moving average voltage.  Support is no longer applied when the voltage stays below tInverters value for the length of the Hold Time.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Gradient for Sags.  Percentage of the rated current to apply capacitively per percentage of the negative deviation from the moving average voltage.   It is a ratio of percent and is therefore unitless.
points.units: percent current per percent voltage deviation

points.description: Dynamic Reactive Current Support Gradient for Swells.  Percentage of the rated current to apply inductively per percentage of the positive deviation from the moving average voltage.   It is a ratio of percent and is therefore unitless.
points.units: percent current per percent voltage deviation

points.description: Dynamic Reactive Current Support Filter Time for Moving Average Voltage.  Used to determine amount of dynamic reactive current support.
Dynamic Reactive Current Support Block Zone Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage below which no reactive current support shall be applied.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Hysteresis Block Zone Voltage.  Percentage of the nominal voltage.   After being blocked, reactive current support shall not resume until the voltage has been above the hysteresis block zone.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Block Zone Time. Time in milliseconds from the beginning of any "sag" event, before which dynamic reactive current support will always continue, regardless of how low voltage may sag.

Dynamic Reactive Current Support Hold Time.   When the voltage returns to witInvertern the deadband limits for tInverters length of time (measured in milliseconds), the "sag" or "swell" event is considered to be over.  Reactive current support ends, frozen values are unfrozen, and a new event can begin.
Dynamic Reactive Current Attempted Output. Current output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the Voltage  input and other parameters.
Dynamic Volt-Watt Voltage Reference Input.  Votltage measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Dynamic Volt-Watt Gradient.  Signed quantity that establishes the ratio of additional Watts supplied to the present difference from the moving average voltage.
points.units: percent watts per percent voltage difference

points.description: Dynamic Volt-Watt Filter Time.  The time in seconds used to calculate the moving average voltage for dynamic Volt-Watt support.

Dynamic Volt-Watt Lower Deadband.  Percentage of the nominal voltage measured below the moving average voltage.  If the present voltage is above tInverters value, no additional Watts shall be supplied.

Dynamic Volt-Watt Upper Deadband.  Percentage of the nominal voltage measured above the moving average voltage.  If the present voltage is below tInverters value, no additional Watts shall be supplied.
Dynamic Volt-Watt Attempted Output.  Watt output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the Voltage  input and other parameters.
Frequency-Watt Frequency Reference Input.  Frequency measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Frequency-Watt High Starting Frequency.  Delta frequency between start frequency and nominal grid frequency for High frequency events.
Frequency-Watt High Stopping Frequency.  Delta frequency between stop frequency and nominal grid frequency for High frequency events.

points.units: percent watts per percent hz difference
percent watts per percent frequency difference

points.description: Frequency-Watt Low Starting Frequency.  Delta frequency between start frequency and nominal grid frequency for low frequency events.
Frequency-Watt Low Stopping Frequency.  Delta frequency between stop frequency and nominal grid frequency for low frequency events.

points.units: percent watts per percent hz difference
percent watts per percent frequency difference

points.description: Frequency-Watt Ramp Up Time Constant. Time constant or open loop response time for moving from the current active power target to a Higher active power target.
Frequency-Watt Ramp Down Time Constant. Time constant or open loop response time for moving from the current active power target to a lower  active power target.

points.units: percent watts per percent hz difference
percent watts per percent hz difference

points.description: Frequency-Watt Attempted Output.  Watt output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the frequency input and other parameters.
Active Power Limit Enabling Time Window. Time window (in seconds) witInvertern which to randomly execute a command. If the time window is zero, the command will be executed immediately
Active Power Limit Enabling Ramp Time. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from current operational mode settings to new operational mode settings

Active Power Limit Reversion Timeout Period. Reversion Timeout Period (in seconds), after which the device will revert to its default status, such as closing the switch to reconnect to the grid or allowing maximum watts output, in case communications are lost or mitigating messages are not received
Active Power Limit Signal Meter ID
Active Power Limit Reference Input.  Active Power measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.

Active Power Limit Minimum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Charge/Discharge Enabling Ramp Time. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from current operational mode settings to new operational mode settings
Charge/Discharge Active Power Target.  Percentage of maximum active power.
Charge/Discharge Ramp Up Time Constant. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from the current active power target to a higher active power target.
Charge/Discharge Ramp Down Time Constant. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from the current active power target to a lower active power target.
Charge/Discharge Minimum Reserve for Storage. The reserve level below which the storage system may only be discharged in emergency situations, expressed as a percentage of the usable capacity.
Charge/Discharge Maximum Reserve for Storage. The reserve level above which the storage system may only be charged in emergency situations, expressed as a percentage of the usable capacity.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Enabling Ramp Time. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from current operational mode settings to new operational mode settings
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Target State of Charge. Charge that the system is expected to achieve, as a percentage of the usable capacity.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Target Date. Date by which the storage system must reach the target SOC.  Days since January 1, 1970, UTC.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Target  Time.  Time by when the storage system must reach the target SOC. Expressed as the number of seconds since the start of Target Date.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Energy Request. Amount of energy that must be transferred from the grid to the charger to move the SOC from the value at the specific time of reference to the target SOC.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Minimum Charging Duration. Minimum duration to move from the SOC at the time of reference to the target SOC.

Coordinated Charge/Discharge Date of Reference.  Date that the SOC is measured or computed by the storage system and is the basis for the Energy Request, Minimum Charging Duration, and other parameters

Coordinated Charge/Discharge Time of Reference.  Time that the SOC is measured or computed by the storage system and is the basis for the Energy Request, Minimum Charging Duration, and other parameters
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Duration at Maximum Charge Rate. Duration that energy can be stored at the Maximum Charge Rate.
Coordinated Charge/Discharge Duration Maximum Discharge Rate.  Duration that energy can be delivered at the Maximum Discharge Rate.

Frequency-Watt Curve Charge Ramp Down Rate

Frequency-Watt Attempted Output.  Watt output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the Frequency input and selected curve. If “Snapshot of Power” is not enabled, this is the maximum active power the outstation will attempt to generate or absorb.
Constant VArs Enabling Ramp Time. Ramp time, in seconds, for moving from current operational mode settings to new operational mode settings.
Constant VArs Reactive Power Target.  Percentage of maximum reactive power.
Constant VArs Ramp Up Time Constant
Constant VArs Ramp Down Time Constant
Constant Power Factor Enabling Time Window
Constant Power Factor Enabling Ramp Time
Constant Power Factor Reversion Timeout Period
Constant Power Factor Setpoint - Generation / Discharging
Volt-VAr Control  Voltage Input.  Voltage measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.

Volt-VAr Control  Adjusted Voltage Reference.  The Voltage used as reference for Volt-VAr control.  If Autonomous Voltage Reference Adjustment is disabled, this is the same fixed value as the Reference Voltage.
Volt-VAr Curve Index.  Index of the Volt-VAr curve that should be used by the mode.
Volt-VAr Autonomous Voltage Reference Adjustment Time Constant
Volt-VAr Attempted Output.  VAr output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the Voltage input and selected curve.
Watt-VAr Reference Power Input.  Power measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Watt-VAr Curve Index.  Index of the Watt-VAr curve that should be used by the mode.
Watt-VAr Attempted Output.  VAr output that the mode is attempting to achieve based on the Watt input and selected curve.
Power Factor Correction Enabling Time Window
Power Factor Correction Enabling Ramp Time
Power Factor Correction Reversion Timeout Period
Power Factor Correction Signal Meter ID
Power Factor Correction Reference Power Factor Input.  Power factor measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Power Factor Correction Average PF Target
Power Factor Correction Lower PF Limit
Power Factor Correction Upper PF Limit
Pricing Mode Setpoint: Hundredths of local currency per Kilowatt-Hr.
Pricing Mode Ramp Up Time Constant
Pricing Mode Ramp Down Time Constant

 <0> Curve disabled
 <1>  Not applicable / Unknown

 <3> Frequency-Watt
 <4> Watt-VAr 
 <5> Voltage-Watt 
 <6> Remain Connected
 <7> Temperature mode
 <8> Pricing signal mode

 <9> HVRT Must Trip

Independent (X-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:
     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / Unknown
     <4> Time
     <29> Voltage
     <33> Frequency
     <38> Watts
     <23>  Celsius Temperature
     <100> Price in hundredths of local currency
     <129> Voltage as a percent of nominal voltage (VRef)
     <133> Frequency as a percent of nominal frequency
     <138> Percent Watts
     <233> Frequency Deviation
     <234+> Other

Dependent (Y-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:
     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / unknown
     <2> VArs as percent of max VArs (VARMax)
     <3> VArs as percent of max available VArs (VArAval)
     <4> Vars as percent of max apparent Watts (VAMax) - used in IEEE 1547
     <5> Watts as percent of max Watts (Wmax)
     <6> Watts as percent of frozen active power (DeptSnptRef)
     <7> Power Factor in EEI notation
     <8> Volts as a percent of nominal voltage (VRef)
     <9> Frequency as a percent of nominal frequency
    <29> Voltage
    <33> Frequency
    <38> Watts
     <99+>  Other

System Meter Type of Connection Point.   Enumeration:
<0> unknown,
<1> DER to local EPS
<2>  Internal to DER
<3> local EPS with load to area EPS (PCC with load)
<4> local EPS w/o load to area EPS (PCC without load)
<5> Load to local EPS
<6> External to DER beyond the PCC
<7>  External to DER within the local EPS
<8> Auxiliary DER Load
<9> Group of DERs to the area EPS
<99> Other

System Meter Type of Circuit Phases.   Enumeration:
<0> unknown
<1> Single phase
<2> Split phase
<3> 2-phase
<4> 3-phase delta
<5> 3-phase wye
<6> 3-phase wye grounded
<7> 3-phase / 3-wire (inverter type)
<8> 3-phase / 4-wire (inverter type)

System Meter Apparent Power Calculation Method.  Calculation method for total apparent power calculation.  Enumeration:
     <0> unknown
     <1> vector
     <2> aritMeteretic
